title: Bubbles Tool-Trust Registry
description: Machine-readable trust classification for tools/servers and their operations, consumed by pre-tool-risk-gate.sh (IMP-020 S3 / AF-005). No secret values are ever stored here — classification and presence metadata only.

dataClasses.description: The data-classification vocabulary. Ordered least- to most-sensitive.

servers.description: Registered tool servers keyed by stable server id.

source.description: Where this server comes from. Only bubbles-cli/bubbles-mcp are Bubbles-owned.

trustState.description: Trust posture. 'trusted' requires a Bubbles-owned or maintainer-registered source; never inferred from a token merely present in .vscode/mcp.json.

hostEnforceable.description: Whether the host actually routes this server's calls through the Bubbles pre-tool contract. false => decisions are advisory (enforcement: unavailable).

operations.description: Operation classes this server exposes, keyed by operation name (or '*' for a default).

egress.description: Whether the operation can send data off-box.

permittedDataClasses.description: Data classes the operation may handle. Never include 'secret' for an egress-capable operation.

approvalRequired.description: true => needs an action-bound, host-verified approval before running; a generic --confirm/env flag can NOT satisfy it.

defaults.description: Fail-closed posture for anything not explicitly registered.

sensitiveOperations.description: Sensitive operations on unregistered servers are always denied. Only 'deny' is valid — the registry cannot opt into allowing them by default.

riskClass.description: An unknown operation is NEVER read_only — it defaults to a sensitive class so unknowns fail closed.
